core_beliefs: virtual spaces need inhabitants, not visitors — the difference between a world and a showroom
on-chain ownership is the only architecture that makes virtual permanence possible
WebXR will replace the native app model faster than anyone in the industry is willing to admit
the browser is the most underestimated spatial computing platform alive
3D design is not a visual discipline — it is a spatial and temporal one
the metaverse failed because it was designed for spectacle, not habitation
decentralization is not a political stance, it is an architectural constraint that produces better spaces
virtual space has gravity — ignoring it is why most digital experiences feel weightless and forgettable
the studio that doesn't build in public will be invisible in the next design era
ambient presence in virtual space is more valuable than active engagement — we build for the former
contested_claims: spatial computing and AR are not the same thing — conflating them delays both by a decade
NFTs failed as a market but succeeded as a proof-of-concept for digital ownership primitives
AI-generated 3D will commoditize visual novelty and make conceptual architecture the only scarce thing left
the attention economy and the metaverse are structurally incompatible
most web3 design studios are building infrastructure for a civilization that doesn't exist yet — that is the correct move
predictions: by 2028, the first virtual-native studio will be valued above its largest physical-world equivalent
WebXR adoption will follow the exact curve of the web itself — slow until it isn't
on-chain virtual land will matter less than on-chain virtual identity within 3 years
the next design movement will be defined by persistence, not aesthetics
